ticket_goal: Implement subtle UI nudges to suggest AI features throughout the application

TECHNICAL_SPECS.Advocate_Perspective: Users need to know AI features exist. Nudges like an 'AutoAwesome' icon or 'Try AI Polish' badge near text inputs can drive engagement. We need a reusable component that adds a gentle visual cue without being intrusive. Tooltips should explain the benefit.
TECHNICAL_SPECS.Skeptic_Perspective: Risk of the 'Clippy' effect if overused. Must allow users to dismiss or disable nudges. If the AI service is down or the user is out of quota, nudges should be hidden. Avoid layout shifts when nudges load.
TECHNICAL_SPECS.Synthesis: Create a reusable <AINudge /> component using MUI AutoAwesome icon. It should support a featureKey to track dismissal state (initially via localStorage or user DB preferences). Integrate primarily in the Post Composer and Media Caption inputs.

Production_Readiness.Logging: Log clicks on the AI nudge to track engagement. Track dismissals.
Production_Readiness.Caching: Cache the 'dismissed' state locally to prevent UI flickering on load.
Production_Readiness.Rate_Limiting: Ensure the nudge action gracefully handles 429 Too Many Requests if the user has exhausted their AI quota, ideally hiding the nudge if quota is known to be zero.

TECHNICAL_SPECS.Dependency_Impact_Radius: Low. Introduces a new UI component and modifies existing text inputs. Uses existing MUI icons and Next.js features.

conditions: src/components/ui/AINudge.tsx component created with Material UI icons.
Nudge integrated into Post Composer or relevant text inputs.

fixes_applied: Created reusable AINudge component with MUI AutoAwesome icon and pulse animation
Implemented localStorage-based dismissal logic in AINudge

Added click and dismissal logging to console.info
Verified implementation with unit tests, type check, and build
